title: Besan Barfi (Gram Flour Fudge)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Ghee (clarified butter) – ¾ cup (150 g), warm & melted
Saffron strands – 6–8, soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk (optional, luxury touch)
Edible silver leaf (varq) – optional, for fine-dining presentation

instruction.title: Prepare Besan Base
steps: Heat a heavy-bottomed kadhai/pan on medium-low.
Roast slowly, stirring continuously, until the mixture turns golden, aromatic, and releases a nutty fragrance (20–25 minutes).
Chef’s Tip: Keep flame low—this ensures even roasting and prevents raw or burnt flavors.

instruction.title: Prepare Sugar Syrup
steps: In a separate saucepan, combine sugar and water.
Cook until it reaches a 1-string consistency (if you press syrup between your thumb and forefinger, it should form a single thread when pulled apart).
Add cardamom powder and saffron milk (if using). Remove from heat.

instruction.title: Combine & Cook
steps: Slowly pour the hot sugar syrup into the roasted besan mixture, stirring continuously.
The mixture will bubble—stir until it thickens and starts to leave the sides of the pan.
The ghee should begin to release slightly, indicating it’s ready.

instruction.title: Set & Garnish
steps: Pour mixture into a greased tray lined with parchment.
Level with a spatula, smooth the surface, and sprinkle with slivered pistachios/almonds.
Optionally, place a sheet of edible silver leaf for a luxe finish.

instruction.title: Cut & Serve
steps: Allow to set at room temperature (1–2 hours).
Once firm, cut into neat squares or diamonds with a sharp greased knife.
Store in an airtight container (lasts up to 10 days at room temp).

chefTips: Sieve the besan before roasting to avoid lumps.
Slow roasting is key—rushing creates a raw taste.
For ultra-smooth texture, pulse the besan once in a grinder before roasting.
Adding saffron & silver leaf elevates it to fine-dining presentation.
Serve on a black slate platter with microgreens or rose petals for a restaurant-style garnish.
